title: Chasing Monsters
subTitle: Fishing enthusiast and adventurer Cyril Chauquet explores the dangerous jungles of South America in search of the brutal Pirarara.

title: Walking with Dinosaurs
subTitle: 66 million years ago, an orphaned baby Triceratops roamed the lush world of Laramidia, alone and vulnerable. Stalked by the fearsome Tyrannosaurus Rex, the youngster relied on her wits.

title: Iceland With Alexander Armstrong
subTitle: Alexander witnesses a volcanic eruption, visits the original geyser, learns about native belief in elves, takes a trip to the Penis museum and meets an Icelandic rock band.

title: Deadly 60
subTitle: Steve Backshall travels to the deadliest desert on the planet, searching for a gruesome savage killer in the form of a tarantula hawk wasp.

title: Deadly 60
subTitle: Steve encounters yellowfin tuna, the duck-billed platypus, the eastern grey kangaroo, a trapdoor spider, the jack jumper ant and the Australian giant cuttlefish.

title: Inside the Factory
subTitle: While Gregg visits a liqueur factory in Ireland that produces 5,40,000 bottles in a day, Cherry explores a recycling plant. Later, Ruth looks at the history of liqueurs.

title: Iceland With Alexander Armstrong
subTitle: Alexander witnesses a volcanic eruption, visits the original geyser, learns about native belief in elves, takes a trip to the Penis museum and meets an Icelandic rock band.

title: Rick Stein's India
subTitle: Rick Stein wanders Mumbai's vibrant streets, tracing the origins of the bombil fish that made Bombay Duck famous, blending culture, cuisine, and captivating coastal flavors.

title: Scandinavia with Simon Reeve
subTitle: Simon travels from the Arctic islands of Svalbard to the icy wilderness of Lapland.

title: Deadly 60
subTitle: Steve Backshall heads to India on a quest to meet the planet's deadliest animals. He is on the trail of the saw-scaled viper, which is said to be the deadliest snake on Earth.

title: Extreme Fishing with Robson Green
subTitle: Robson heads to the Tennessee River in the Deep South of the US to try catfish grabbling. This perilous sport involves catching giant catfish without using rods.

title: Iceland With Alexander Armstrong
subTitle: Alexander witnesses a volcanic eruption, visits the original geyser, learns about native belief in elves, takes a trip to the Penis museum and meets an Icelandic rock band.

title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
subTitle: Rick Stein visits Iceland's coastal capital, Reykjavik, where he takes a walk by the harbour in order to find the perfect cod and experiences a hearty breakfast of waffles with rhubarb jam and cream.

title: Chasing Monsters
subTitle: In Polynesia, Cyril tries to catch a legendary fish known to the locals as the Uruati, but he is not prepared for the incredible number of sharks lurking below the waters.

title: Scandinavia with Simon Reeve
subTitle: Simon travels from the Arctic islands of Svalbard to the icy wilderness of Lapland.

title: Walking with Dinosaurs
subTitle: Dazzling computer animation highlights this six-hour "speculative documentary" following the lives of dinosaurs from their beginning to their zenith -- and their eventual decline during the Cretaceous period. The computer-generated imagery and animatronics used in the series show dinosaurs in a way you would expect to see in a feature film.
